text.title: Client Visibility vs Velocity Tradeoff Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Client Visibility vs Velocity Tradeoff. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Classify clients into 3 tiers: HIGH (>$25K + multi-month + executive sponsor), MID ($5-25K), LOW (<$5K or one-off). Visibility budget scales by tier.
rule.rationale: Without tiers, every client gets equal visibility, which is the bug.

rule.statement: Each tier gets fixed cadence: HIGH=weekly call+daily Slack, MID=weekly email+as-needed Slack, LOW=biweekly email, no Slack DM.
rule.rationale: Fixed cadence kills ad-hoc pings; client knows when to expect update.

rule.statement: Each ad-hoc touch costs ~30 min context-switch. >5 touches/client/week = velocity tax >2.5 hrs.

rationale.source: Cal Newport Deep Work + practitioner data

rule.rationale: Slack/email interruptions cost 30 min recovery time on average; sum to real number.

rule.statement: Status update is templated: shipped / in-progress / blockers / next-week / risks. Prose-only updates expand by 3x.
rule.rationale: Template caps update time; un-templated update inflates with conversation.

rule.statement: Quarterly: re-tier every client. Tiers shift as contracts expand/shrink or risk profile changes.
rule.rationale: Static tiers miss shifts; quarterly revisit aligns visibility to current reality.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-client-visibility-vs-velocity-tradeoff.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
